Start with the payment

A comfortable budget is more useful than a maximum approval.

See principal, interest, taxes, insurance, HOA, and estimated mortgage insurance together. Then decide whether the payment fits your actual life.

  • Use editable taxes and insurance instead of a generic national average.
  • Compare down-payment choices side by side.
  • Save your scenario or ask Dennis to review it.

Why clients call Dennis

“You should understand every number, every tradeoff, and every next step before you feel pressured to move.”

Dennis brings the discipline of fifteen years of Navy service and the calm of a trained social worker to a process that can otherwise feel confusing. You get a direct conversation, a plain-language recap, and options shaped around your file.

Meet Dennis →Read Google reviews →

A calmer process

From curious to confident in three steps.

Start small. Share more only when the next step makes sense.

Three decisions. One clear path.

Your starting point

Useful, not generic

The blog turns real questions into useful next steps.

Dennis is continually publishing Florida-specific answers about VA, FHA, self-employed income, insurance, equity, and the decisions buyers face right now.

Questions before the call

Get the concern out of the way, then decide what deserves a closer look.

These are the questions most likely to stop a borrower from taking the next step. The full library goes deeper without turning the homepage into a mortgage textbook.

Search every answerAsk Dennis directly →
No blanket promises.Answers explain the framework and show what still depends on the actual file.
Can I explore my mortgage options without a credit check?

Yes. The pathfinder and calculators can organize your goals, payment assumptions, and likely questions without a credit pull. A formal preapproval is different: it normally requires your authorization, a credit review, and supporting information before a lender can make a credit decision.

Start the 60-second pathfinder →Open the calculators →
What happens after I send Dennis my scenario?

Dennis reviews the goal, timing, rough payment range, and the questions that could change the path. You can then choose whether to keep planning, book a conversation, or move into the secure application. Starting the conversation does not obligate you to apply.

Build my starting plan →Book a conversation →
How much cash might I actually need to buy a home?

Cash to close can include the down payment, lender and third-party closing costs, prepaid taxes and insurance, the initial escrow deposit, inspections, and sometimes reserves. The number changes with the loan, property, closing date, seller credits, and any eligible assistance, so it should be modeled as a complete scenario instead of guessed from the down-payment percentage alone.

Model a purchase payment →Explore DPA options →
If my mortgage rate is fixed, can my total monthly payment still change?

Yes. On a typical fixed-rate mortgage, scheduled principal and interest generally remain stable, but the complete housing payment can change when property taxes, homeowners or flood insurance, mortgage insurance, or escrow requirements change. HOA dues are another housing cost and are often paid separately.

Model the complete payment →
How should I compare FHA, VA, conventional, and USDA financing?

Compare eligibility, down payment, mortgage insurance or funding fees, rate and APR, property requirements, loan limits, cash to close, and the complete monthly payment. The lowest advertised rate or smallest down payment is not automatically the lowest-cost or best-fitting option for your timeline.

Compare mortgage paths →Run program-specific calculators →
What credit score do I need for a mortgage?

There is no single website number that answers this reliably. Program rules, automated underwriting findings, lender overlays, credit history, debt-to-income ratio, reserves, property, occupancy, and loan purpose can all matter. Dennis can explain which paths may be worth reviewing without promising an approval from a score alone.

Organize my starting scenario →

Already know what you need?

Move directly to the secure application.

Use this path when you are ready to share income, assets, employment, and credit information.
